barRaise.topFinding: Eleventh consecutive daily review with zero application-code findings and the fourth straight day with no commits (HEAD still 3d9b480 / v0.18.4, last moved 2026-06-14). The 06-18 canonical audit reads fully aligned; the one prior LOW is already corrected in the working tree and sits uncommitted by design. The only material movement is in the work queue, the wrong direction: the apothecary footprint grew from three items to five across four project-ID spellings, with a new mirror-stale-tree item blocked-on-user. The three MEDIUMs are unchanged and none is a bug: the 864 MB .git, the queue-ID sprawl, and a 98%-complete tool still on a daily cadence. The highest-leverage move is overdue for a sixth review: cut v1.0, settle the .git call, normalize the queue, and drop Apothecary to weekly review.

actions.title: Decide and close the 864 MB `.git` bloat: `git filter-repo --strip-blobs-bigger-than 5M` + force-push, or a "Won't fix" locked decision in PROJECT_SPEC section 8.
Cut v1.0, mark "Symbol + label polish" done, and drop the project to weekly review. Sixth review carrying this.

Normalize the queue to one project ID (`yaa`) with a `prompt` field, close the shipped `yaa-commit-release-automsg-2026-06-12`, and clear the new YaE-side mirror-stale-tree and pill-bump items. (Orchestration side; read-only here.)
